data.title: This data is genuinely useful — it changes
data.body: This data is genuinely useful — it changes the eligibility problem from "show users their limit" to "actively guide them toward the right products for their specific limit." Let me think through this properly.

data.title: The Core Insight From the Data
data.body: **The Core Insight From the Data**

data.title: The most important number here isn't the average
data.body: The most important number here isn't the average — it's that **56% of employees sit between ₹1L and ₹4L**, and the largest single segment (20.3%) is ₹2L-₹3L. This means your default product experience should be optimized for this band, not for the ₹5L+ Deloitte user who is probab
data.annotation: The most important number here isn't the average — it's that **56% of employees sit between ₹1L and ₹4L**, and the largest single segment (20.3%) is ₹2L-₹3L. This means your default product experience should be optimized for this band, not for the ₹5L+ Deloitte user who is probably already motivated.

data.title: The second insight is the catalog gap above
data.body: The second insight is the **catalog gap above ₹3.65L**. 17.7% of employees have limits above ₹4L but there's no single product that fills that space. These users are most at risk of under-utilizing their benefit — not because they don't want to spend it, but because the catalog d
data.annotation: The second insight is the **catalog gap above ₹3.65L**. 17.7% of employees have limits above ₹4L but there's no single product that fills that space. These users are most at risk of under-utilizing their benefit — not because they don't want to spend it, but because the catalog doesn't give them an obvious way to.

data.title: These two insights should drive completely different interventions.
data.body: These two insights should drive completely different interventions.

data.title: 1. Home screen — personalized hero messaging
data.body: **1. Home screen — personalized hero messaging**

data.title: Right now the home screen is generic for
data.body: Right now the home screen is generic for everyone. But a user with ₹80,000 limit and a user with ₹5,00,000 limit have completely different shopping realities. The hero section and the "Devices for you" section should reflect this.

data.title: For ₹50K-₹1L users, the messaging should be about
data.body: For ₹50K-₹1L users, the messaging should be about flagship accessibility — "Your benefit gets you a premium phone." This segment might not realize their limit reaches iPhone 15 or Pixel 9 territory. Surfacing that clearly changes the perception of the platform.

data.title: For ₹2L-₹3L users (the largest segment), the message
data.body: For ₹2L-₹3L users (the largest segment), the message is about combinations — "Your benefit covers a phone and a laptop." This is the combo opportunity you identified in the data.

data.title: For ₹4L+ users, the message is about bundles
data.body: For ₹4L+ users, the message is about bundles and ecosystems — "Build your complete work setup." Without this framing, these users have no obvious way to spend their full limit.

data.title: 2. A "Built for your limit" section on
data.body: **2. A "Built for your limit" section on home screen**

data.title: Below the hero, instead of or alongside "Devices
data.body: Below the hero, instead of or alongside "Devices for you," a section that shows 2-3 curated options specifically calculated to use the user's limit well. Not just products within their limit — products that together *approach* their limit meaningfully.

data.title: The logic: find the combination of available products
data.body: The logic: find the combination of available products that gets closest to their total limit without exceeding it. Show that as a "Recommended for you" bundle. This is the smart bundle idea made concrete.

data.title: 3. Catalog — limit-aware category entry points
data.body: **3. Catalog — limit-aware category entry points**

data.title: When a user enters the catalog with a
data.body: When a user enters the catalog with a ₹1.2L limit, the category pills could show estimated product counts within their limit — "Phones (166)" rather than a generic count. This helps them understand the platform is sized for them.

data.title: More importantly, users in the ₹1L-₹1.5L band need
data.body: More importantly, users in the ₹1L-₹1.5L band need to know they can reach MacBook Air M2 territory. This probably isn't their first assumption. A subtle callout — "Your limit reaches laptops" — within the catalog could completely change their consideration set.

data.title: 4. Cross-category discovery nudges
data.body: **4. Cross-category discovery nudges**

data.title: This is the most underexplored opportunity in the
data.body: This is the most underexplored opportunity in the data. Most users probably think about one device at a time. But the data shows clear combo opportunities — especially for the ₹2L-₹3L band where MacBook Air + iPad mini, or iPhone Pro + Apple Watch, fit neatly.

data.title: These combos should be surfaced as named bundles
data.body: These combos should be surfaced as named bundles with a combined price displayed against the limit. "iPhone 15 Pro + Apple Watch Series 10 = ₹2,28,000 — within your ₹2,50,000 limit." The user doesn't have to do the math; you've done it for them.

data.title: 5. The ₹4L+ catalog gap problem
data.body: **5. The ₹4L+ catalog gap problem**

data.title: This needs its own intervention because it's a
data.body: This needs its own intervention because it's a structural issue, not just a messaging one. Three approaches:

data.title: First, surface bundle-first for these users on the
data.body: First, surface bundle-first for these users on the home screen — skip the single-product browse entirely and lead with "Complete setups for your ₹{limit}." This reframes the catalog from "expensive products you can't find" to "curated setups sized for you."

data.title: Second, show progressive bundle building — "Start with
data.body: Second, show progressive bundle building — "Start with MacBook Pro 14", add iPad Pro, add AirPods Max — that's ₹4,85,000 of your ₹5,00,000." Guide them step by step toward maximization.

data.title: Third, consider whether the catalog gap is a
data.body: Third, consider whether the catalog gap is a product catalog problem worth flagging to the business — 3,793 employees with limits above ₹4L and no single product above ₹3.65L is significant revenue being left on the table.

data.title: 6. PDP — "What else fits after this"
data.body: **6. PDP — "What else fits after this"**

data.title: Once someone selects a product on the PDP,
data.body: Once someone selects a product on the PDP, the remaining headroom calculation tells you exactly what combinations are possible. A section at the bottom of the PDP — "With ₹{remaining} left, you could also add" — turns a single product decision into a bundle discovery moment. This
data.annotation: Once someone selects a product on the PDP, the remaining headroom calculation tells you exactly what combinations are possible. A section at the bottom of the PDP — "With ₹{remaining} left, you could also add" — turns a single product decision into a bundle discovery moment. This is more contextual and less pushy than a generic accessory recommendation because it's explicitly tied to their remaining benefit.

data.title: 7. Limit-tier specific empty states
data.body: **7. Limit-tier specific empty states**

data.title: When users in lower limit bands (₹50K-₹1L) first
data.body: When users in lower limit bands (₹50K-₹1L) first open the platform and see flagship phones priced at ₹1.5L+, the platform might feel like it's not for them. The untouched state for these users needs different messaging — lead with what's achievable within their specific limit, no
data.annotation: When users in lower limit bands (₹50K-₹1L) first open the platform and see flagship phones priced at ₹1.5L+, the platform might feel like it's not for them. The untouched state for these users needs different messaging — lead with what's achievable within their specific limit, not with the most premium products in the catalog.

data.title: The Prioritization Question
data.body: **The Prioritization Question**

data.title: Before designing any of these, I'd want to
data.body: Before designing any of these, I'd want to know two things:
